fields.name: cloudprofiler_projects_profiles_list
fields.description: Lists profiles which have been collected so far and for which the caller has permission to view.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: Optional. The maximum number of items to return. Default page_size is 1000. Max limit is 1000.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Optional. The token to continue pagination and get profiles from a particular page. When paginating, all other parameters provided to `ListProfiles` must match the call that provided the page token.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: ListProfilesResponse
types.description: ListProfileResponse contains the list of collected profiles for deployments in projects which the user has permissions to view.

fields.name: profiles
fields.description: List of profiles fetched.

fields.name: nextPageToken
fields.description: Token to receive the next page of results. This field maybe empty if there are no more profiles to fetch.

fields.name: skippedProfiles
fields.description: Number of profiles that were skipped in the current page since they were not able to be fetched successfully. This should typically be zero. A non-zero value may indicate a transient failure, in which case if the number is too high for your use case, the call may be retried.

types.name: Profile
types.description: Profile resource.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Output only. Opaque, server-assigned, unique ID for this profile.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Duration of the profiling session. Input (for the offline mode) or output (for the online mode). The field represents requested profiling duration. It may slightly differ from the effective profiling duration, which is recorded in the profile data, in case the profiling can't be stopped immediately (e.g. in case stopping the profiling is handled asynchronously).

fields.name: profileBytes
fields.description: Input only. Profile bytes, as a gzip compressed serialized proto, the format is https://github.com/google/pprof/blob/master/proto/profile.proto.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Input only. Labels associated to this specific profile. These labels will get merged with the deployment labels for the final data set. See documentation on deployment labels for validation rules and limits.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: Output only. Start time for the profile. This output is only present in response from the ListProfiles method.

types.name: mutation_cloudprofiler_projects_profiles_create_profileType
types.description: Type of profile. For offline mode, this must be specified when creating the profile. For online mode it is assigned and returned by the server.

types.name: Deployment
types.description: Deployment contains the deployment identification information.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: Project ID is the ID of a cloud project. Validation regex: `^a-z{4,61}[a-z0-9]$`.

fields.name: target
fields.description: Target is the service name used to group related deployments: * Service name for App Engine Flex / Standard. * Cluster and container name for GKE. * User-specified string for direct Compute Engine profiling (e.g. Java). * Job name for Dataflow. Validation regex: `^[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9_.]{0,253}[a-z0-9])?$`.

fields.name: labels
fields.description: Labels identify the deployment within the user universe and same target. Validation regex for label names: `^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?$`. Value for an individual label must be <= 512 bytes, the total size of all label names and values must be <= 1024 bytes. Label named "language" can be used to record the programming language of the profiled deployment. The standard choices for the value include "java", "go", "python", "ruby", "nodejs", "php", "dotnet". For deployments running on Google Cloud Platform, "zone" or "region" label should be present describing the deployment location. An example of a zone is "us-central1-a", an example of a region is "us-central1" or "us-central".

types.name: JSON
types.description: The `JSON` scalar type represents JSON values as specified by [ECMA-404](http://www.ecma-international.org/publications/files/ECMA-ST/ECMA-404.pdf).

types.name: Byte
types.description: The `Byte` scalar type represents byte value as a Buffer

fields.name: cloudprofiler_projects_profiles_create
fields.description: CreateProfile creates a new profile resource in the online mode. _Direct use of this API is discouraged, please use a [supported profiler agent](https://cloud.google.com/profiler/docs/about-profiler#profiling_agent) instead for profile collection._ The server ensures that the new profiles are created at a constant rate per deployment, so the creation request may hang for some time until the next profile session is available. The request may fail with ABORTED error if the creation is not available within ~1m, the response will indicate the duration of the backoff the client should take before attempting creating a profile again. The backoff duration is returned in google.rpc.RetryInfo extension on the response status. To a gRPC client, the extension will be return as a binary-serialized proto in the trailing metadata item named "google.rpc.retryinfo-bin".

fields.name: cloudprofiler_projects_profiles_createOffline
fields.description: CreateOfflineProfile creates a new profile resource in the offline mode. The client provides the profile to create along with the profile bytes, the server records it. _Direct use of this API is discouraged, please use a [supported profiler agent](https://cloud.google.com/profiler/docs/about-profiler#profiling_agent) instead for profile collection._

fields.name: cloudprofiler_projects_profiles_patch
fields.description: UpdateProfile updates the profile bytes and labels on the profile resource created in the online mode. Updating the bytes for profiles created in the offline mode is currently not supported: the profile content must be provided at the time of the profile creation. _Direct use of this API is discouraged, please use a [supported profiler agent](https://cloud.google.com/profiler/docs/about-profiler#profiling_agent) instead for profile collection._

args.name: updateMask
args.description: Field mask used to specify the fields to be overwritten. Currently only profile_bytes and labels fields are supported by UpdateProfile, so only those fields can be specified in the mask. When no mask is provided, all fields are overwritten.

types.name: CreateProfileRequest_Input
types.description: CreateProfileRequest describes a profile resource online creation request. The deployment field must be populated. The profile_type specifies the list of profile types supported by the agent. The creation call will hang until a profile of one of these types needs to be collected.

inputFields.name: profileType
inputFields.description: One or more profile types that the agent is capable of providing.

types.name: Deployment_Input
types.description: Deployment contains the deployment identification information.

inputFields.name: projectId
inputFields.description: Project ID is the ID of a cloud project. Validation regex: `^a-z{4,61}[a-z0-9]$`.

inputFields.name: target
inputFields.description: Target is the service name used to group related deployments: * Service name for App Engine Flex / Standard. * Cluster and container name for GKE. * User-specified string for direct Compute Engine profiling (e.g. Java). * Job name for Dataflow. Validation regex: `^[a-z0-9]([-a-z0-9_.]{0,253}[a-z0-9])?$`.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Labels identify the deployment within the user universe and same target. Validation regex for label names: `^[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]{0,61}[a-z0-9])?$`. Value for an individual label must be <= 512 bytes, the total size of all label names and values must be <= 1024 bytes. Label named "language" can be used to record the programming language of the profiled deployment. The standard choices for the value include "java", "go", "python", "ruby", "nodejs", "php", "dotnet". For deployments running on Google Cloud Platform, "zone" or "region" label should be present describing the deployment location. An example of a zone is "us-central1-a", an example of a region is "us-central1" or "us-central".

types.name: Profile_Input
types.description: Profile resource.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Output only. Opaque, server-assigned, unique ID for this profile.

inputFields.name: duration
inputFields.description: Duration of the profiling session. Input (for the offline mode) or output (for the online mode). The field represents requested profiling duration. It may slightly differ from the effective profiling duration, which is recorded in the profile data, in case the profiling can't be stopped immediately (e.g. in case stopping the profiling is handled asynchronously).

inputFields.name: profileBytes
inputFields.description: Input only. Profile bytes, as a gzip compressed serialized proto, the format is https://github.com/google/pprof/blob/master/proto/profile.proto.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Input only. Labels associated to this specific profile. These labels will get merged with the deployment labels for the final data set. See documentation on deployment labels for validation rules and limits.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: Output only. Start time for the profile. This output is only present in response from the ListProfiles method.

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
